topic: Kristal Solar Park vs. floating PV
claim_in_report: Kristal Solar Park is presented as floating solar on post-mining quarry lakes.
finding: Kristal Solar Park (99.5 MW, operational 2019) is a ground-mounted installation on the Kristalpark III industrial estate in Lommel, supplying green power to nearby industry including Nyrstar. A documented floating PV precedent in the same Sibelco sand-extraction context is the 7 MWp Floating PV project on the De Schans quarry lake at Mol/Dessel.

claim_in_report: Lake Victoria deposits match or approach Lommel-grade purity (>99.5% SiO₂).
finding: DGSM and historical BGS/NORA reports document very high SiO₂ at specific sites (e.g. up to ~99.95% at Kome; ~99.93% delineated at Diimu/Bukakata). Deposit heterogeneity, grain size, and processing requirements mean site-specific assays are required before equating to Lommel zilverzand specifications.

claim_in_report: Mining and Minerals Act should enforce reclamation bonds.
finding: The Mining and Minerals Act, 2022 requires mine closure plans, progressive rehabilitation, and financial assurance (bonds/guarantees). Amendment discussions (2026) may strengthen bonding — cite the enacted Act for current law.
